page: Microsoft Azure D48d_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the DDv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, with hyper-threading enabled at 2.0 threads per core and dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It includes 1843 GB of local NVMe storage, but does not feature any GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong capabilities in specific workloads. The instance achieves a PassMark CPU score of 48327.07 and a PassMark memory score of 2751.93, both placing in the top 25% tier. Database operations on PassMark scored 21519.8, also in the strong category, while Redis SET operations reached 44.26 million operations per second. In terms of processing, Geekbench image processing scored 14999.0 (top 25%), and CPU encryption tests scored 42548.75. For language model tasks, text generation using a small 135M model reached a strong 285.96 tokens per second, though larger models like the 70B model dropped to an average 2.81 tokens per second.

From a resource density perspective, the D48d_v5 offers a balanced ratio of compute to memory alongside a substantial local storage allocation. The inclusion of 1843 GB of local disk space provides high-speed local scratch space without requiring external storage attachments, enhancing qualitative cost efficiency for disk-intensive operations. However, the lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, and the 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio may not suffice for memory-intensive in-memory databases that require larger RAM footprints.

This instance is suited for relational database management systems, enterprise application servers, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate-scale data processing. The strong performance in database operations and local storage capacity makes it effective for workloads requiring fast local read/write operations and balanced CPU-to-memory resources.
description: Microsoft Azure D48d_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the DDv5 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It features 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) running at 2.8 GHz, 192.0 GB of RAM, and 1843 GB of local storage, with no GPU accelerators. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance demonstrates strong benchmark performance, particularly in PassMark database operations, CPU encryption, and image processing, all ranking in the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth reaches up to 2499.33 GB/sec for cached small blocks. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and large local storage make this instance qualitatively cost-effective for workloads requiring local scratch space. It is suited for relational databases, enterprise application hosting, software compilation, and web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48d_v5 is a general-purpose VM featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 1843 GB local storage. It delivers strong database and encryption performance on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48d_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 1843 GB local storage, optimized for database workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 compute instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-capacity local storage, and strong database processing capabilities.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores on a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor.
192.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio.
1843 GB of integrated local storage for high-speed scratch space.
Strong benchmark performance in database operations, encryption, and image processing.
Suited for relational databases, enterprise application servers, and software compilation.
